word: G1278 - διαπονέω - diaponéō - dee-ap-on-eh'-o - Be grieved

sections.paragraph:
- Original: διαπονέω

- Transliteration: Diaponeo

- Phonetic: dee-ap-on-eh'-o

- Definition:

1. to work out laboriously, make complete by labour

a. to exert one's self, strive

b. to manage with pains, accomplish with great labour

c. to be troubled, displeased, offended, pained, to be worked up

- Origin: from G1223 and a derivative of G4192

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G1223 and a derivative of G4192; to toil through that is (passively) be worried: - be grieved.
